days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring the bustling streets

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: After passing through customs, head to the AEL (Airport Express Line) to Central Station for a smooth journey into the city.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong International Airport, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel ICON
timeline.detail: Drop your bags at this luxurious hotel located in Tsim Sha Tsui. It offers stunning Victoria Harbour views and exceptional facilities.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Walk along Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade
timeline.detail: Stroll along the promenade with spectacular views of Hong Kong Island. Perfect for catching the sunset and enjoying the cityscape.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Aussie BBQ
timeline.detail: Indulge in hearty Australian BBQ dishes while enjoying the vibrant atmosphere of Tsim Sha Tsui. Don’t miss the steak and lamb chops.
timeline.mapQuery: Aussie BBQ, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: A Symphony of Lights Show
timeline.detail: Witness the spectacular light and sound show featuring the skyscrapers of Hong Kong. Best viewed from the promenade.

timeline.title: Nightcap at Ozone Bar
timeline.detail: Finish your day with a drink at one of the highest bars in the world. Enjoy stunning city views and a sophisticated atmosphere.

localTips: Buy a Octopus Card at the airport for convenient transport access.
Check the schedule for the Symphony of Lights to catch the show.
Try local street food at the nearby markets after dinner.

days.title: Explore Kowloon
days.subtitle: Markets and cultural experiences

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Enjoy a classic Hong Kong breakfast of toast, eggs, and milk tea at this local favorite known for its speedy service.
timeline.mapQuery: Australia Dairy Company, Jordan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit the Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Explore this famous temple known for its fortune telling. Experience the spirituality and beautiful architecture of this historic site.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Wong Tai Sin, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore the Ladies' Market
timeline.detail: Shop for souvenirs and bargain for clothes and accessories in this vibrant street market. Don’t forget to haggle!
timeline.mapQuery: Ladies' Market, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Taste authentic Hong Kong wonton noodles at this renowned eatery. Their handmade noodles and seafood wontons are a must-try.

timeline.title: Visit the Hong Kong Museum of History
timeline.detail: Learn about Hong Kong's rich history and culture through interactive exhibits. Ideal stop for history lovers wanting to understand the city better.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong Museum of History, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Dine at the Michelin-starred dim sum restaurant. Their baked buns and steamed dumplings are especially popular, so get there early.
timeline.mapQuery: Tim Ho Wan, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

highlights: Dim sum at Tim Ho Wan
localTips: Take the MTR for quick access to all locations, especially during peak hours.
Carry cash for market shopping and small eateries.
Check the MTR schedule to plan your return to the hotel.

days.title: Lantau Island Adventure
days.subtitle: Nature and culture

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel ICON
timeline.detail: Start your day with a hearty breakfast at the hotel's buffet that offers both local and international cuisines.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Take the Ngong Ping 360 Cable Car
timeline.detail: Enjoy breathtaking views of Lantau Island aboard the Ngong Ping cable car. Experience the scenic ride up to the Big Buddha.
timeline.mapQuery: Ngong Ping 360, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit the Big Buddha
timeline.detail: Stand in awe of the massive 34-meter-tall statue of Buddha. Explore the Po Lin Monastery nearby for beautiful architecture and tranquil gardens.
timeline.mapQuery: Tian Tan Buddha, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Po Lin Monastery Vegetarian Restaurant
timeline.detail: Savor a unique vegetarian meal influenced by Buddhist cuisine, surrounded by serene natural beauty.
timeline.mapQuery: Po Lin Monastery Vegetarian Restaurant, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Tai O Fishing Village
timeline.detail: Visit this historic village with stilt houses and experience the traditional fishing culture of Hong Kong. Don’t forget to try some local snacks.
timeline.mapQuery: Tai O Fishing Village, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and Relax
timeline.detail: Head back to the hotel after a day full of exploration. Unwind and prepare for the evening.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Farewell Dinner at Jumbo Kingdom
timeline.detail: End your trip with a memorable dinner at the floating restaurant Jumbo Kingdom. Enjoy Cantonese cuisine while surrounded by stunning views.

localTips: Check cable car weather conditions to ensure operation.
Plan for extra time to explore Tai O village.
Make reservations for Jumbo Kingdom if possible.

hotels.name: Hotel ICON
hotels.note: Modern hotel offering stunning harbor views, 5-min walk from MTR
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Road, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.item: Return flight from Penang to Hong Kong

budget.note: For convenient transportation around the city
Tasting local delicacies in the village

urgent.label: Book Ngong Ping 360 Cable Car
urgent.note: Advance booking recommended for better timing
